explicit_text: I, Clarence J. Thornton, was born on March 12, 1898, in San Francisco, California. I am the only child of Michael Thornton and Margaret Joyce. My early life was shaped by the legacy of my family, who had deep roots in California and Ireland. Though my mother passed away when I was just two years old, I grew up knowing the stories of my grandfather and father.

explicit_text: My grandfather, Michael Thornton, was born in County Cork, Ireland, in 1839 and died in Merced, California, in 1924. He came to America about a year before the Civil War ended and was conscripted into the Army despite not being a citizen. He settled in Merced County as a grain farmer and raised a large family with my grandmother Ellen. His life bridged Ireland and California, and his story deeply influenced our family.

explicit_text: John William Thornton was my grandfather Michael Thornton’s brother who came to America on the same ship from Ireland. Like my grandfather, he settled in Merced County, California, and was a grain farmer. His life closely paralleled my grandfather’s and was an important part of our family history.
